suggestion: The topic is publishable as-is at CoG and competitive at IJCAI. To strengthen for AAAI main track, narrow the headline claim: instead of trying to bridge CGT and MARL simultaneously (which risks being shallow on both), pick one anchor. Recommended: lead with 'connectivity-constrained spatial games defeat CGT decomposition — here is the first empirical characterization' (SQ1+SQ2), then use RL agents as a tool to probe game structure rather than as a co-equal contribution. This avoids the reviewers' likely objection that 4GB VRAM self-play on 15x15 is under-powered relative to AlphaZero-era expectations. The power-cell disruption analysis (SQ3) is the strongest differentiator from generic 'we trained RL on a board game' papers — promote it from P4 to P2. The 8-player coalition analysis (SQ6) should be cut entirely; the research report itself says alliances barely matter in this game, and claiming emergent coalition dynamics without evidence invites skepticism.
